mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and reduced household consumption capacity, which depresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, which amplifies near-term corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 20-day returns are negative in >60% of co-occurring expansion events.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product directly increases aggregate corporate revenue streams, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the inflation signal is consistently overridden by liquidity injections without return degradation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal produces negative returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when tested against a univariate PAYEMS model.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a late-cycle expansion phase signals peak consumer exhaustion and impending demand contraction, which forecasts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 60-day returns in >65% of cases, or the signal fails to distinguish late-cycle from early-cycle dynamics.
